beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and make coffee at home while checking weather and work Slack.
beats.reasoning: Low energy (45/100) from yesterday carries into a slower morning start. Drizzly weather makes an early commute less appealing; Tomas checks the SR-520 closure alert and decides to delay departure.

beats.activity: Breakfast: toast, eggs, and second coffee while reviewing the day's security audit agenda.
beats.reasoning: Risk-averse planner eats at home to avoid rain and stay focused before the commute; no need to travel for breakfast.

beats.activity: Drive to Renton workplace, taking I-5 south to avoid the SR-520 eastbound closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closed eastbound 9am–noon; Tomas leaves early enough to take I-5 alternate and arrive before the peak closure window. Rainy conditions add 10–15 min buffer.

beats.activity: Security audit and client briefing at Renton office.
beats.reasoning: Core work block in the morning; the SR-520 closure peak (9am–noon) overlaps, so staying at the office is the prudent choice rather than attempting to return to Seattle mid-day.

beats.activity: Lunch at a nearby café in Renton; grab a sandwich and coffee to go.
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady at midday (peak until 2pm); Tomas avoids driving back to Seattle and eats locally to save time and stay dry.

beats.activity: Return to office work: finish audit report and client documentation.
beats.reasoning: Post-lunch work block; rain clears after 2pm, but Tomas stays focused on wrapping up the audit to avoid a rushed evening. Low energy suggests a measured, steady afternoon.

beats.activity: Drive back to Seattle via I-5, now clear of the SR-520 closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure has ended (noon deadline); rain is clearing. Tomas heads home with enough daylight left to relax before evening.

beats.activity: Stop at QFC for groceries: pick up brewing supplies and fly-fishing magazine.
beats.reasoning: On the way home; Tomas grabs supplies for his home-brewing hobby and reads about fly-fishing routes—aligns with his downtime interests and low energy (prefers routine, familiar tasks).

beats.activity: Walk home from QFC, enjoying the clearing skies.
beats.reasoning: Rain has stopped; a short walk grounds Tomas after a long day in the office and clears his head before settling in for the evening.

beats.activity: Home: tidy up, organize brewing gear, and read about Cascade fly-fishing routes.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down beat at home; low energy and risk-averse personality favor a quiet evening tending to hobbies rather than socializing. Feeds his interest in fly-fishing and home brewing.

beats.activity: Prep for tomorrow, light tidying, and wind-down routine before bed.
beats.reasoning: Final home beat before sleep; Tomas plans-ahead as a risk-averse consultant and ensures tomorrow is set up smoothly.
8-hour sleep ending at 06:30 next morning; low energy from yesterday and a full day at the office warrant solid rest at home.

diary: Woke to that familiar Seattle grey—the kind that makes you grateful for hot coffee. Got through the morning routine on autopilot, checking Slack while the rain hammered outside. The SR-520 closure meant a detour south on I-5, which actually worked out fine. The audit went smoothly; our client seemed satisfied with the findings. Grabbed a sandwich in Renton and powered through the afternoon documentation. By the time I drove back, the rain had stopped and the sky was breaking up a little. Picked up some brewing supplies at QFC—been meaning to try a new pale ale recipe. The walk home felt good after sitting in the office all day. Spent the evening organizing my gear and reading about fly-fishing routes up in the Cascades. There's something settling about planning a trip you haven't taken yet.
thought_process: I routed around the bridge closure without much stress—it's part of traveling for work, you learn to adapt. The audit felt routine today, which is actually a good sign; no surprises means solid security posture. I think the clearing weather in the afternoon lifted my mood more than I expected. The grocery stop wasn't planned urgently, but I grabbed the brewing supplies because I've been putting off that hobby restart. Reading about Cascade routes felt like a small escape—I travel constantly for clients, but that's different from traveling for myself. By evening I was in a reflective headspace, thinking about the difference between work trips and real trips, between solving other people's security problems and just... being in nature.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
